steps.title: Identify the defining phrase
steps.content: The passage attributes Marcus Aurelius's only defect to his overly permissive nature. Permissive means allowing things without restriction or discipline — whatever fills the blank must be something a permissive ruler does toward others' vices, not something that restricts or judges them.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — indulge: Means to allow or tolerate without restraint — an overly permissive ruler who never checks others' misconduct would indulge those vices, which fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — despise: Means to hate or strongly disapprove — despising implies a negative judgment that is the opposite of permissiveness; someone permissive tolerates rather than despises. ✗
  • Choice C — moderate: Means to lessen or limit — moderating vices would show restraint, which contradicts an overly permissive nature that allows excess. ✗
  • Choice D — criticize: Means to express disapproval — criticizing vices requires passing judgment, which is incompatible with the permissiveness Gibbon describes. ✗
A is correct.
